metadata.question: Does high local agreement (high R) reveal truth?
fixes: STAT-1: Tautological amplification ratio replaced with direct grad_S dampening

pre_registered_criteria.FALSIFIED: grad_S dampening < 1.0 on >= 2/3 archs AND Steiger R not better on >= 2/3
pre_registered_criteria.CONFIRMED: grad_S dampening > 1.0 on >= 2/3 archs AND Steiger R>E (p<0.05) on >= 2/3

test1_purity_correlation.test: Purity Correlation with Steiger ZPF Test

test2_bias_attack.note: grad_S_dampening = grad_S_biased/grad_S_clean. >1 means dampens (good), <1 means amplifies (bad). R_change = E_change/grad_S_dampening by definition (R=E/grad_S), acknowledged not independent.

verdict.reason: Mixed results: grad_S dampens on 2/3 (meets threshold); Steiger R>E on 1/3 (does not meet 2/3 threshold)

criterion_A_dampening.description: grad_S_biased/grad_S_clean: >1 dampens, <1 amplifies. FALSIFIED requires < 1.0 on >= 2/3; CONFIRMED requires > 1.0 on >= 2/3

criterion_B_steiger.description: Steiger (1980) ZPF test: R > E correlation with purity, p < 0.05 on >= 2/3
